name: Hau'ula Loop Trail
tagline: A lush windward ridge loop that most tourists never find — all the Norfolk pines and ocean views without the crowds.
parking: Free lot at Hau'ula Homestead Road trailhead — small lot, maybe 10 cars
bestTime: Morning for the clearest views — afternoon clouds move in fast on the windward side
talkStory: This 2.5-mile loop gains about 600 ft of elevation and takes most people about 90 minutes. Go counterclockwise for the best experience — you get the steeper climb out of the way first and finish with the gentle ridge walk. Great views of La'ie and the windward coast. The Norfolk pine groves are beautiful and provide solid shade. A perfect 'easy day' hike when you want nature without the commitment.
